steps.title: Fastest path: identify the total first
steps.content: Use the total volume and the known component volume before setting up equations. Since the mixture volume is the sum of the two component volumes, write the needed subtraction directly.

steps.title: Calculate Total Volume
steps.content:
The total volume of the mixture is given as 46 mL. The mixture consists of both isopropanol and water.
The volume of isopropanol in the mixture is 13 mL.

steps.title: Determine Water Volume
steps.content:
To find the volume of water, subtract the volume of isopropanol from the total mixture volume:
$$46\text{ mL} - 13\text{ mL} = 33\text{ mL}.$$
The answer is 33.
